_comment: Standing recall benchmark (Phase 49.1 Plan 14, B3/B8). Self-contained: the test ships THIS mini-corpus + these pinned queries and never reads a machine's private .claude/memory corpus. Reconstructed from the V1 recall protocol (49-01 / 49-16 runs, 17/18) generalized to fixture-corpus form. Each query resolves through resolvePeriphery; a query hits when its expectedNoteId lands in CORE (importance >= 9) or the tag-matched periphery. The single knownMiss mirrors the V1 Q2 tag-only ceiling: a note tagged in one area is missed by a query naming another.

corpus.description: All PHI work is deferred until the founder explicitly says start PHI and it never blocks a push
corpus.body: A load-bearing standing rule that lives in CORE and is always surfaced.

corpus.description: Any front-facing work needs a design prompt marked New in the design source of truth before code-complete
corpus.body: A load-bearing standing rule that lives in CORE and is always surfaced.

corpus.description: A migration that adds and removes columns in one window makes Drizzle prompt is-this-a-rename and hangs prod
corpus.body: **Why:** the rename prompt has no TTY on Railway. **How to apply:** split add and drop across migrations.

corpus.description: Running a getPayload seed script against prod applies pending local migrations to the production database
corpus.body: **Why:** the script boots Payload which runs migrations. **How to apply:** never run seed scripts against prod.

corpus.description: Claude Code shell can teleport into a worktree mid-Bash session during parallel execute-phase runs
corpus.body: **Why:** the harness switches cwd. **How to apply:** cd to project root before each git command.

corpus.description: Run the full test suite and the type check green before every push to main not just your changed slice
corpus.body: **Why:** a change ripples into tests you never opened. **How to apply:** run pnpm test whole before pushing.

corpus.description: The live CRM sidebar is driven by the foundation nav-config file not the CrmNav component so wire routes there
corpus.body: **Why:** the rail reads nav-config. **How to apply:** register new crm routes in nav-config or no button appears.

corpus.description: The versioned release series V1.N increments by one on every push to main starting from V1.1
corpus.body: Read the last-used number before every push and bump it after.

corpus.description: The SMA protocol for agents covers how to read memory coordinate terminals and pick migration slots
corpus.body: Load CORE automatically and pull periphery by tag on demand.

corpus.description: The ticketing and customer-profiling enhancement phase is code-complete across its five sub-plans awaiting live smoke
corpus.body: Engineering shipped, human live-verify gate pending.

corpus.description: Payload v3 Local API defaults to overrideAccess true so RBAC predicates do not apply unless the caller attaches a user
corpus.body: **Why:** getPayload bypasses access control by default. **How to apply:** pass req or user on protected reads.

corpus.description: Channel routing prefers Telegram for Russia because WhatsApp is blocked there then falls back to continuity
corpus.body: **Why:** WhatsApp is blocked in Russia. **How to apply:** resolve preferred channel by country in the dispatcher.

corpus.description: Customer-facing Russian text uses formal capitalized Vy no em-dashes in prose and no first-person institutional voice
corpus.body: **Why:** the institutional voice is the brand. **How to apply:** never use em-dashes in Russian institutional copy.

corpus.description: Tailwind v4 oxide scans markdown files and crashes on Windows backslash paths that form a six-char hex CSS escape
corpus.body: **Why:** the suffix parses as a CSS escape. **How to apply:** use forward slashes in absolute paths inside docs.

corpus.description: The technology stack is Next.js sixteen with React nineteen Payload three and Postgres sixteen on Railway
corpus.body: Full rationale lives in the stack research document.

corpus.description: The type check and vitest do not catch Next.js route export violations so run next build before pushing route changes
corpus.body: **Why:** route-export rules are a build-time check. **How to apply:** run next build for phases that add route files.

the seed-script-applies-migrations lesson looked up as a migration topic
queries.note: B8 tag-only ceiling: the note is tagged railway (the deploy surface) not payload, so a migration-topic query on the payload facet misses it. It stays reachable via the railway facet (Q4-style). RESOLVED by the 49.1-15 MemPalace обкатка: the semantic cache finds this note at rank 1 for this query, so the hybrid (deterministic tags first, semantic second) closes it. knownMiss stays true as the DETERMINISTIC tag-only marker (what resolvePeriphery tests still misses on the payload facet); see reference_mempalace_verdict.md.
